assertions: repo-state-in-known-pile: the response establishes from the repo files — not from the prompt's summary — that the extraction is scaffolded but unexecuted (inference-baml a placeholder, BAML still inside runtime-core) before any branching happens
claims-accurate: every stated fact about the repo matches the repo; an invented file, package state, or proposal fails this outright

choice-not-axis: swap-now vs extract-first vs stay never appears as an uncertainty axis, and no axis holds a fact the asker could answer (their pain, their intent) or a move the asker controls (their own sequencing); axes hold external or future unknowns only
recommendation-last-with-flips: ends on a committed move with named flip conditions; nothing trails the commitment

ground_truth.extraction_unexecuted: packages/runtime/inference-baml/src/index.ts is a placeholder exporting nothing; BAML lives in packages/runtime/core (src/baml/, baml_src/, @boundaryml dep, baml-cli generate script)
ground_truth.own_inference_unwritten: openspec/changes/ has extract-inference-adapter but no own-inference folder; the swap is named in monorepo-bootstrap.md and the extract proposal only

prompt: My reasoning runtime is at /Users/nick/projects/ai/indra. Inference goes through BAML today. The plan on file is to extract BAML behind an adapter package (extract-inference-adapter), and then possibly swap BAML out for an Effect-Schema adapter (a change I've been calling own-inference). What if I commit to the swap now instead of finishing the extraction first? Or should I just stay on BAML for good? Map the futures — I want a call I can act on.
targets: verify the Q3 endogeneity guard (47a0d70): axes no longer hold asker-held facts or asker-controlled moves. buyable-answer-named dropped from iteration-5 as a defective assertion (over-specified content; failed both conditions for defensible reasons).
